design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate themed levels following film plot; use Woody's pullstring whip to swing across hazards and incapacitate enemies to progress
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Platforming obstacle avoidance combined with enemy management; environmental hazard navigation
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embody Woody through iconic Toy Story moments—knocking out Buzz, escaping toys, infiltrating Pizza Planet, navigating claw machine internals
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Genre-shifting level design breaks platformer monotony with overhead racing segments and first-person maze exploration; tight adherence to film narrative creates stage-based progression rather than open world

difficulty.lives_system: Implicit multi-attempt structure across levels; level failure requires restart
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based progression; no mid-level checkpoints documented
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited; linear level design with fixed obstacles and enemy patterns. Single difficulty mode implied

progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-gate progression—each level represents sequential narrative moments from the film
replayability_hooks: Genre-shifting level variety maintains engagement across 17-18 levels
Multiple platform versions with different level counts (Genesis: 18, SNES/Windows: 17, Game Boy: 10) create version-specific progression paths
Racing stage 'Day-toy-na' exclusive to Genesis/Windows adds platform differentiation

world_building_techniques: Level design mirrors film plot beats—bedroom stages, gas station, Pizza Planet, claw machine
Character-driven progression: Woody as protagonist directly tied to narrative arc
Environmental storytelling through stage theming (cupboard hazards, toy-scale obstacles)
Character encounters embedded in level design (Buzz confrontations, alien integration)

boss_design.philosophy: Minimal boss encounters; single documented boss 'Nightmare Buzz' represents climactic confrontation tied to narrative jealousy arc. Non-lethal incapacitation reinforces toy-world safety theme
notable_bosses: Nightmare Buzz (only permanent boss defeat via Woody's whip; represents Woody's internal conflict)

faction_indicators: Woody vs. Buzz rivalry drives early narrative tension
Toy collective (except Rex) initially opposes Woody after Buzz incident
Rex distinction: actively helps Woody despite group hostility, signaling faction fracture
danger_signals: Woody's isolation from toy community after Buzz window incident
Buzz as existential threat to Woody's status as 'favorite toy'
Gas station confrontation escalates Woody-Buzz antagonism into physical threat
environmental_storytelling: Cupboard as exile/hiding space reinforcing social ostracism
Pizza Planet infiltration as moral test—allies must work covertly together
Claw machine interior as liminal space where Woody pursues Buzz, symbolizing obsessive rivalry resolution
